concerns.text: Phase 6 (WYSIATI Mechanism) introduces a four-way interaction term (fixation × valence × CRT × confidence_time) and a new variable `confidence_assignment_time` that are not defined in spec.md (FR-004 specifies only a three-way interaction) or plan.md. This creates a semantic dependency violation: the regression model (T024) is defined to output a three-way interaction, but Phase 6 tasks (T039) require it to output a four-way interaction. The consumer (T039) depends on a producer (T024) that, as currently defined, does not produce the required artifact structure.

Data flow violation in synthetic data generation: T022 generates `belief_rating` and `cognitive_reflection_score`, T023 caps outliers in `cognitive_reflection_score`, but T037 generates `confidence_assignment_time` based on `cognitive_reflection_score`. The tasks are split across Phase 4 and Phase 6 without a clear dependency ensuring T023 (capping) executes before T037 (correlation calculation). If T037 runs on uncapped data, the correlation logic is flawed. T037 must explicitly depend on the completion of T023.

Producer/Consumer ordering risk: T021 (Valence calculation) requires headline text. T022 (Synthetic Data Generator) creates the dataset containing `belief_rating` and `cognitive_reflection_score`. The current ordering implies T021 might run on raw gaze data (which lacks text) before T022 merges the headline text into the dataset. T021 must be ordered after the data merge step (implied in T022 or a missing merge task) to ensure the text input exists.

Ambiguous dependency in T032: 'Implement sensitivity analysis for the fixation duration cutoff over a range of significance levels'. This conflates the data threshold (50/100/150ms) with the statistical alpha level. The task description is unclear on whether it depends on the robustness report (T034) or the regression results (T027). Clarify if this is a re-run of the regression with different alpha thresholds or a post-hoc analysis of T034.
